questions.question: According to the Revenue Per Subscriber (RPS) benchmark for solo creators, what is the target monthly RPS range?
questions.explanation: The benchmark for solo creators is $1-$3 per subscriber per month. If you have 2,000 subscribers and earn $0.10/subscriber/month, you have a conversion problem, not an audience problem.

questions.question: What is the 'Content Hamster Wheel' failure mode?

options.text: Creating content that is too short to be useful
Publishing constantly but never selling anything, creating goodwill but no commercial relationship
Reposting the same content across multiple platforms
Outsourcing content creation to AI tools without editing

questions.explanation: The Content Hamster Wheel is when a creator publishes constantly but never sells anything. Their content creates goodwill but no commercial relationship -- essentially operating as a free media company.

questions.question: Why does moving UP the monetization spectrum (from ad revenue toward coaching/consulting) deliver exponentially more revenue?

options.text: Higher-priced offers require less marketing effort
Algorithms favor creators who sell high-ticket products
Each step up requires more trust but delivers exponentially more revenue per fan
Consulting clients always refer more new customers than course students

questions.explanation: Every step up the monetization spectrum requires more trust but delivers exponentially more revenue. A creator with 5,000 followers selling a $497 course to 2% of their email list earns more than a creator with 500,000 followers relying on ad revenue.

questions.question: Which of the five failure modes (Content Hamster Wheel, Audience-Offer Mismatch, Platform Trap, Trust Deficit, or Price Floor) most applies to your current creator business? Describe specifically how it shows up in your day-to-day operations and what you plan to do differently after this lesson.
questions.aiPrompt: Evaluate if the student demonstrates honest self-assessment of their monetization challenges and identifies a specific failure mode with concrete examples from their own business. Look for actionable next steps rather than vague intentions.
